message.content: Adversaries may gather information about a victim's network to facilitate targeting and subsequent attacks. This information can include IP ranges, domain names, network topology, and operational details. Common methods for collecting this data include Active Scanning, Phishing for Information, and searching open technical databases. Such reconnaissance can reveal opportunities for further actions like Active Scanning, acquiring or compromising infrastructure, and establishing initial access through trusted relationships. For instance, the HAFNIUM group collected fully qualified domain names (FQDNs) of targeted Exchange servers. Mitigation strategies include network segmentation, regular monitoring for unusual scanning activities, and educating users on phishing risks to reduce the likelihood of information exposure.
